ogTitle: State Mine Gully vs Steam Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in State Mine Gully and Steam Plains.
heading: State Mine Gully vs Steam Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between State Mine Gully, New South Wales and Steam Plains, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Steam Plains has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to State Mine Gully (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is State Mine Gully or Steam Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: State Mine Gully has a flood risk score of 59/100, while Steam Plains scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: State Mine Gully scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Steam Plains scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in State Mine Gully or Steam Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Steam Plains has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
